title: docs/mkdocs: integrate interactive API references
description:
Replace the DGX Cloud Platform docs API reference tables with kubectl-doc interactive schema views generated from CRDs.

Scope:
- Build a side-by-side, unlinked API reference tree in dgxcloud/platform/docs before replacing the existing pages.
- Generate all public resources listed in scripts/allowed-kinds.txt, not just Blueprint.
- Source schemas from controllers/infra generated CRD manifests under apis/manifests/crds/gen.
- Use the shared kubectl-doc web runtime or a thin React/custom-element wrapper, with one rendering/interaction code path.
- Keep existing links stable when the replacement is promoted.
- Support lazy full-schema loading for large resources.
- Preserve interactive behavior: folding, focus details, keyboard navigation, filtering, semantic comment wrapping, required/default/validation styling, and valid copyable YAML.
- Account for the Platform docs build using the GitLab MkDocs component, which injects the theme/base config during CI.

Non-goals:
- Do not improve the current proto table renderer except where needed as a migration bridge.
- Do not ship a Markdown-only replacement.
design: Prefer the shared non-React kubectl-doc web runtime for MkDocs unless the GitLab MkDocs component makes a compiled React/custom-element bundle cleaner. In either case, the renderer/runtime logic must be shared with kubectl-doc html/web and Fern, not forked. Use generated asset manifests plus per-resource shallow/full schema payloads so pages are static and do not need live OpenAPI fetches.
acceptance_criteria:
- A generator creates a parallel, unlinked interactive API reference tree for every allowed public resource.
- Generated pages build in the Platform docs MkDocs pipeline and can be previewed on GitLab Pages.
- Each resource page embeds an interactive kubectl-doc viewer sourced from CRD schema data.
- Large schemas render quickly with initial shallow data and lazy full-schema loading.
- Existing api-reference pages remain untouched during the spike.
- Promotion plan is documented for replacing existing pages while preserving URLs.

title: preserve details overlay through lazy schema remount
description: Fern/Dynamo side-overlay details can disappear after lazy full-schema loading. The shared web runtime destroys and remounts the schema DOM, removing kdoc-has-focus; if the host remains the active element, no focusin event fires and side-overlay details stay hidden.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking a field before lazy full-schema loading keeps the details overlay visible after the full payload remounts. Subsequent field clicks still show details. Covered by Fern/React Playwright regression.
close_reason: Preserved side-overlay focus state across lazy full-schema remounts in the shared web runtime and React wrapper, with a Fern/React regression covering details visibility after full payload loading.

title: filtering keeps fold controls interactive
description: Filtering currently reveals matching schema paths through a separate visible-set projection that ignores fold state. In web/html/Fern and likely TUI, clicking or keying expand/collapse while a filter is active changes state but does not reliably affect visibility. Filter mode must still allow fold/unfold and then reevaluate the filtered projection.
acceptance_criteria: While a schema filter is active, triangle clicks and left/right/enter folding controls can collapse and expand matching branches in web/html/Fern and TUI. Matches inside previously collapsed branches are auto-revealed, Escape restores the pre-filter folded state except focused ancestors, and Enter accepts the revealed/current fold state. Regression tests cover TUI and shared web runtime contracts.
close_reason: Fixed filter-mode fold projection in shared web runtime and TUI: filtering now snapshots fold state, auto-reveals matching paths, keeps user fold overrides interactive, restores on Escape, and accepts/toggles correctly on Enter with regression coverage.

title: Fix Fern lazy schema remount state
description: Fern schema view regresses after lazy full-schema loading: expanding beyond the shallow depth briefly opens then collapses again, keyboard/focus state becomes inconsistent, root description comment lines are selected independently, and filtering can collapse semantic wrapping into one-word comment lines after the full payload arrives. Fix the shared runtime/Fern adapter and propagate to Dynamo.
close_reason: Fixed Fern full-schema preload/cache behavior, preserved fold/filter snapshots across lazy remounts, grouped root description lines, regenerated full Dynamo-scale fixtures, and propagated the consumer update to Dynamo.

title: Fix Fern schema selection and fold clicks
description: Dynamo Fern preview can leave multiple highlighted schema fields after mixed click/keyboard focus, and fold triangles are not clickable even though keyboard left/right updates collapse state. Fix shared runtime and propagate generated Fern artifacts.
close_reason: Fixed stale Fern selection cleanup and capture-phase fold click handling; propagated runtime adapter behavior to tests.

title: render/fern: move schema sidecars off markdown routes
description: Fern page routes can block for seconds even for 100-200 KB schema payloads. Move generated full schema payload sidecars to JSON-only static assets and remove the page-route sidecar transport from generator, component loader, docs, and downstream Dynamo integration.
close_reason: Fern full schema sidecars now emit and load JSON static assets via fullPayloadURL; the React adapter fetches response.json(), and tests cover sidecar generation/loading.

title: Keep metadata in shallow Fern payloads
description: Initial Fern/web shallow payloads must include the full metadata subtree while keeping it collapsed. Expanding metadata should not require fetching the multi-megabyte full schema sidecar.
close_reason: Kept metadata descendants in shallow Fern payloads, hid collapsed Fern lines correctly, and covered metadata expansion without full sidecar fetch.

title: Expand collapsed shallow schema nodes after lazy load
description: Fern/web shallow schema payloads omit children below initially collapsed nodes. Expanding a collapsed node such as metadata must load the full payload and preserve the requested expanded state so ObjectMeta fields appear.
close_reason: Fixed shared runtime lazy expansion for shallow Fern/web payloads: expanding collapsed metadata now loads and remounts the full schema while preserving expanded/filter state; added Fern browser regression coverage.

title: Add local Fern React preview harness
description: Add a local Vite/React harness in kubectl-doc that imports the repo-owned Fern component, loads generated schema sidecars, and verifies Fern-like rendering without Dynamo CI.
close_reason: Implemented shared tokenized schema rendering, local Fern React preview harness, Dynamo consumption, and verified refreshed hosted Fern preview.

title: Fix Fern shared runtime token rendering
description: Fern preview renders shared kubectl-doc YAML token markup as broken text. Fix the shared runtime/adapter so generated YAML tokens render as DOM nodes in Fern while preserving HTML renderer behavior.
close_reason: Implemented shared tokenized schema rendering, local Fern React preview harness, Dynamo consumption, and verified refreshed hosted Fern preview.

title: Speed up web filtering highlights for large schemas
close_reason: Implemented indexed web filter visibility and match-only highlighting.

title: Fix web filter regression after cached state
close_reason: Fixed web filter highlight cleanup for multi-character queries, made filter overlay sticky, and added Escape quit behavior for explicit resource views.

title: Create Homebrew tap repository
description: Create sttts/homebrew-tap, publish the kubectl-doc formula for the current release, and verify brew can install from the tap.
close_reason: Created public sttts/homebrew-tap, published kubectl-doc 0.1.2 formula, verified brew install/test, and added release workflow automation for future formula updates.

title: Fix krew-index git authentication
description: The release workflow krew-index clone step sends a bearer header for git-over-HTTPS and fails with could not read Username. Use GitHub-compatible basic auth for git operations.
close_reason: Fixed in b1eddc7 by using a GitHub-compatible basic auth extraheader for krew-index git clone/fetch/push.

title: Implement path-aware schema filtering
description: Implement dotted JSON-path schema filters with root anchoring, ellipsis wildcards, and substring tail matching in TUI and web schema views.
close_reason: Fixed in aff15c9 by implementing path-aware schema filters in TUI and web schema views with tests and generated HTML updates.

title: Document path-aware schema filtering
description: Extend filtering requirements with dotted JSON-path matching, ellipsis wildcards, root anchoring, and substring tail semantics.
close_reason: Fixed in ca9e7ee by documenting path-aware schema filtering syntax and highlight behavior.

title: Avoid field-name highlights for description-only filters
description: Filtering should include fields whose descriptions match, but must only highlight the actual visible query text; description-only matches should not highlight unrelated field names.
close_reason: Fixed in 1b6378a by highlighting only visible query matches and removing description-only field-name highlight fallbacks.

title: Prevent TUI panic on empty filter results
description: Filtering to no schema matches can leave selectedIndex at -1 and schemaView slices with an invalid range.
close_reason: Fixed in d22994e by clearing focus/top for empty TUI filter results and rendering an empty schema pane safely.

title: Highlight filter matches in field names
description: Filtering on a field name such as kind matches the row via metadata/description but does not visually highlight the key text itself in the HTML output.
close_reason: Fixed in d22994e by highlighting field names for all direct HTML filter matches.

title: Fix GitHub Actions release workflow failures
description: Update release/pages artifact actions for Node.js 24 compatibility and avoid gh repo clone GraphQL access in the Krew index branch push step.

title: Native schema model validation pass
description: Validate kubectl-doc's internal Structural model against all native Kubernetes resources and identify required extensions beyond the copied CRD Structural shape.
design: After the first native OpenAPI renderer works, iterate through built-in resources from OpenAPI v3 fixtures or a cluster. Each unsupported/lossy schema case should become a small explicit extension to internal/schema rather than arbitrary OpenAPI support.
acceptance_criteria: A broad native-resource fixture/test pass exists. Unsupported native schema cases are documented with diagnostics or implemented as concrete Structural extensions. The project still rejects arbitrary OpenAPI v3 support outside Kubernetes structural schema rules.
notes:
Native schema pass boundary: do not support OpenAPI composition quantors as renderer structure. Keep only Kubernetes int-or-string detection via x-kubernetes-int-or-string or format int-or-string, and validate native coverage against real /openapi/v3 group fixtures.
Observed a real cluster where discovery advertises core/v1 Pods but /openapi/v3/api/v1 omits Pod schemas. kubectl-doc now resolves resource schemas from component GVK metadata first, then operation GVK metadata, and finally falls back to embedded upstream native OpenAPI v3 for missing built-in schemas in the same group-version.
Live apps/v1 Deployment exposed another native schema case: field schemas can carry description/default plus generated single-ref allOf to the real component schema. Treat that exact allOf shape as a reference wrapper and keep general OpenAPI composition unsupported.
Added renderer-level native fixture sweep: embedded OpenAPI v3 GVK schemas are converted through the kube adapter, rendered as YAML at bounded depth, parsed back as YAML, and the apps/v1 Deployment regression asserts spec expands to selector/template instead of spec: {}.
close_reason: Validated the available embedded Kubernetes OpenAPI v3 fixtures through conversion and YAML rendering, added regression coverage for Deployment spec expansion, and implemented concrete native/schema extensions found during the pass: incomplete native OpenAPI fallback, generated single-ref allOf wrappers, and field-local examples.

title: Cluster resource resolution and OpenAPI v3 schemas
description: Resolve kubectl get-style resource selectors from discovery, fetch the selected OpenAPI v3 group-version document, and render native Kubernetes resource schemas through kubectl-doc's internal Structural model.
design: Start with discovery-backed REST/resource matching, ambiguity diagnostics, and latest-version auto-selection for non-interactive modes. Then fetch /openapi/v3 entries and adapt native schemas into the internal Structural model, extending that model only for concrete native-resource cases found during fixture coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Selectors such as deployments, deployments.apps, and deployments.v1.apps resolve deterministically or report ambiguity. Cluster schema rendering fetches OpenAPI v3 only, never v2. Native resources render through the same YAML renderer as CRDs. Tests cover selector resolution, ambiguity, OpenAPI v3 fetching, and representative built-in resources.
close_reason: Implemented discovery-backed RESTMapper selector resolution, OpenAPI v3 index/document fetching, native schema rendering through kubectl-doc Structural, and tests for selector grammar, ambiguity, OpenAPI v3 fetching, and representative apps/batch built-in fixtures.

title: Cluster discovery resource overview
description: Add the first in-cluster path for kubectl-doc: when no CRD file is passed and no resource is selected, load kubeconfig/discovery data and print a deterministic resource overview. This intentionally stops before cluster OpenAPI schema rendering.
design: Use client-go deferred kubeconfig loading and discovery. Build a small internal overview model from served APIResourceLists, grouping resources by API group and collecting served versions. Keep schema rendering on the existing CRD path.
acceptance_criteria: kubectl doc with no -f and no resource loads cluster discovery and prints groups, resources, and served versions. Output is deterministic and excludes subresources. Resource selectors without -f return a clear not-implemented error for schema rendering. Unit tests cover overview construction, rendering, and CLI branching.
close_reason: Implemented cluster discovery overview: client-go loading, deterministic resource/version overview, CLI no-file branch, and tests.

title: MVP: CRD YAML docs generator
description: Implement the first kubectl-doc milestone: initialize the Go/Cobra project and support CRD-file input rendered as deterministic YAML documentation through an internal Kubernetes Structural schema model copied from the CRD structural shape.
design: Start with CRD file mode only. CRD OpenAPI v3 schemas are converted through Kubernetes' CRD structural helper into kubectl-doc's own Structural model. Renderers depend only on the internal model, so later cluster OpenAPI support can adapt native resources without depending on CRD backend types. Do not implement cluster OpenAPI fetching, TUI, browser, man, or Markdown renderers in this milestone.
acceptance_criteria: kubectl doc -f <crd.yaml> [--version v] -o yaml prints syntactically valid YAML; Cobra flags are wired; renderers consume kubectl-doc's internal Structural model; the CRD backend adapts Kubernetes CRD structural schemas into that model; focused golden tests cover the output.
close_reason: Implemented CRD-file YAML MVP with Cobra CLI, internal Structural schema adapter, and golden tests.

title: web/runtime: remove duplicate Fern runtime source
description: Make the shared web runtime truly single-source. Today the Fern-facing runtime file is generated from internal/render/web/assets/kubectl-doc.js, but the repository still contains a second checked-in JS copy for Fern packaging. Replace that with a packaging path that cannot be edited independently, or add a hard CI drift check that treats it strictly as generated output until Fern bundling can consume the shared asset directly.
acceptance_criteria: There is one authoritative runtime source for web behavior; Fern consumes that source via generation or packaging only; CI fails if any Fern-facing runtime or style artifact drifts from the shared source; docs and tests state that Fern may not implement separate line rendering, filtering, folding, focus, details, syntax highlighting, or comment wrapping.
close_reason: Removed the checked-in Fern runtime copy, moved the reusable adapter to react/kubectl-doc, made make gen create the ignored runtime bundler artifact from the single web runtime source, and updated tests/docs for the generic React adapter.

title: Hide Fern wrap toggle
description: Fern embeds should always wrap schema comments and should not render the interactive wrap toggle. Keep standalone HTML/web behavior unchanged.
close_reason: Fern component now hides the wrap control and keeps semantic comment wrapping enabled by default; added browser coverage for the no-toggle behavior.

title: Speed up web filtering and reflow
close_reason: Cached web schema line/filter/comment state so large resources filter and wrap without quadratic scans or repeated comment reflows.

title: Add jsonschema YAML output format
close_reason: Added -o jsonschema plain JSON Schema YAML output.

title: Wrap YAML comments to interactive terminal width
close_reason: Explicit YAML output now wraps comments to --columns or interactive terminal width.

title: Share terminal colors between YAML and TUI renderers
close_reason: Shared terminal styles and aligned TUI overview colors with YAML terminal colors.

title: Preserve TUI colors after filter match highlights
close_reason: TUI filter highlighting now reapplies the active ANSI SGR sequence after highlighted matches so comment/key/scalar colors continue after the orange span.

title: Show root schema description before apiVersion
close_reason: Rendered root schema descriptions as standalone comments before apiVersion across tree-backed outputs and Kro, with tests and regenerated examples.

title: Enable Krew release workflow
description: Turn the commented Krew release automation into an active GitHub Actions job that renders the doc manifest, pushes it to the krew-index fork, and attempts to open an upstream PR.
close_reason: Enabled the release workflow job that renders doc.yaml and pushes a Krew manifest branch to the krew-index fork; PR creation remains commented for manual use.

title: Document Krew install and future release automation
description: Add README Krew install instructions and commented release workflow notes for rendering a Krew manifest and opening a krew-index PR from a fork.
close_reason: Added README Krew install command and commented future krew-index PR automation in the release workflow.

title: Prepare Krew packaging files
description: Add Apache-2.0 licensing and a Krew doc.yaml manifest draft for kubectl-doc, and ensure release archives include the license file.
close_reason: Added Apache-2.0 license, Krew doc manifest draft, and license inclusion in release archives.

title: Document Homebrew installation
description: Add a README installation section showing the fully qualified Homebrew tap install command for kubectl-doc.
close_reason: Added README installation section with Homebrew tap command.

title: Add tagged binary release workflow
description: Add GitHub Actions automation that builds kubectl-doc binaries for tagged releases across Linux, macOS, and Windows and publishes them as release assets.
close_reason: Added tag-triggered GitHub Actions release workflow for Linux, macOS, and Windows binaries.

title: Resource field filter in interactive modes
description: Add incremental filtering for the selected resource/schema view in both TUI and web/browser outputs. Typing in resource view should immediately filter visible fields; this is separate from modal search. Browser-native search and TUI slash search remain search commands, while plain typing starts or updates the filter.
acceptance_criteria: TUI schema view and web schema view filter visible fields as the user types. A field remains visible when the field name contains the filter substring, the field description contains it, any parent field name contains it, or any parent description contains it. Ancestors needed to keep the filtered tree readable remain visible. The current filter string is shown as a lightweight overlay at the top. Esc clears the filter and restores the unfiltered folded view as far as practical. Filtering starts on plain typing and does not use /; / keeps its existing search behavior in TUI and browser-native search remains available in web. Tests cover name matches, description matches, parent matches, parent-description matches, Esc clearing, and navigation over filtered visible fields in TUI and web.
notes:
Esc behavior: first Esc exits/clears active resource-field filtering or search state. If no filter/search state is active in an opened schema, the next Esc returns from schema view to the overview while preserving overview focus where practical.
Filtering highlight requirement: resource-field filtering must visually highlight the matched substrings in visible field names and descriptions in both TUI and web. Use the existing strong orange search highlight style where practical, while keeping the current selected field/cursor readable.
Escape focus requirement: when Esc clears resource-field filtering, the cursor/focus must remain on the same logical field that was focused while filtering, as long as that field still exists in the schema. Ancestors should be expanded or otherwise made visible as needed so the focused field remains reachable after clearing.
Filtering visibility requirement: resource-field filtering must evaluate the full logical schema tree, not only fields that are currently unfolded/visible. If a collapsed descendant matches, the filtered view must reveal the full ancestor path to that field so matching fields inside collapsed subtrees can be reached. Clearing the filter should restore the previous fold state as far as practical while keeping focus on the selected logical field.
close_reason: Implemented interactive schema filtering in TUI and web: plain typing filters the logical schema tree including collapsed descendants, reveals matching paths, highlights matches, preserves focus and fold semantics on Esc/Enter, supports direct-match Tab navigation, and has renderer tests.

title: Overview filter in interactive modes
description: Add incremental filtering for resource overview mode in both TUI and web/browser outputs. Typing in overview mode should filter visible API groups and resources by the current input string without entering schema view. The active filter should be shown as a lightweight overlay at the top of the overview. Esc clears the filter and restores the unfiltered overview.
acceptance_criteria: TUI overview filters API groups and resource/version rows as the user types; web overview does the same in the browser overview. The current filter string is visible as a top overlay in both modes. Esc clears the filter in both modes. Existing navigation continues to work over the filtered result set, including up/down resource movement, left/right and tab/shift-tab group jumps, enter selection, and preserving/restoring overview focus where practical. Tests cover TUI and web overview filtering, overlay rendering, Esc clearing, and navigation over filtered results.
notes:
Esc behavior: first Esc exits/clears active overview filtering or search state. If no filter/search state is active and a schema is open, the next Esc returns from schema view to the overview while preserving overview focus where practical.
Filtering highlight requirement: overview filtering must visually highlight the matched substrings in visible API group names, resource names, and version labels in both TUI and web. Use the existing strong orange search highlight style where practical, while keeping the current selected row/cursor readable.
Filtering match requirement: overview filtering must also match Kubernetes resource shortname aliases, not only API group names, resource names, and version labels. For example, filtering by a shortname such as "po" should keep pods visible. Tests should cover shortname matches in both TUI and web overview filtering, and the visible resource/version row should highlight the matched alias where it is shown or otherwise highlight the matching resource row clearly.
Escape focus requirement: when Esc clears overview filtering, the cursor/focus must remain on the same logical resource/version item that was focused while filtering, as long as that item still exists in the unfiltered overview. It must not jump back to the previous pre-filter position or the first visible item.
close_reason: Implemented interactive overview filtering in TUI and web: plain typing filters groups/resources/versions/shortnames, highlights matches, preserves logical focus on Esc, supports group navigation over filtered rows, and has TUI/web tests.

title: Improve HTML syntax highlighting and field details
description: Make the HTML renderer use CSS-based YAML syntax highlighting, tighter line spacing, and schema-backed details without YAML comment markers. Details should include validations and Kubernetes extensions.
acceptance_criteria: HTML output has CSS token spans and customizable variables; line spacing is tighter; selected field details come from schema metadata, not rendered YAML comments; details include defaults, enum, validations, extensions, and descriptions; generated examples are refreshed.
close_reason: Implemented CSS-based HTML YAML highlighting, schema-backed field details, grouped field selection, and search key handling.

title: Use non-trivial README example CRD
description: Switch the generated README GitHub Markdown example from the tiny stable.example.com CRD to a realistic non-trivial CRD fixture.
acceptance_criteria: make gen renders a non-trivial CRD example in README and docs/examples; CI generated-file checks still pass; stale mini example output is removed.
close_reason: README and docs/examples now use a richer DynamoGraphDeployment CRD fixture

title: Kro SimpleSchema renderer
description: Add -o kro to render selected schemas in Kro SimpleSchema-style YAML.
acceptance_criteria: -o kro renders CRD and cluster resources using Kro SimpleSchema-style type/marker syntax; supports --all-versions; preserves Kubernetes-only metadata as comments or details; tests cover scalars, defaults, enums, arrays, maps, required fields, and descriptions.
close_reason: Implemented -o kro SimpleSchema-style rendering for CRD and cluster schemas, all-version YAML streams, Markdown field-details opt-in, generated README example updates, and docs/tests.

title: Generated GitHub README example and CI checks
description: Add a GitHub Markdown example generated by make gen, document it in README, and add GitHub CI checks for generation drift, Go tests, and golangci-lint.
acceptance_criteria: make gen updates the checked-in GitHub Markdown example/README content; CI runs make gen and fails on dirty generated files; CI runs go test ./... and golangci-lint for formatting/linting.
close_reason: Added generated GitHub Markdown example, make gen/check-generated targets, GitHub CI, pinned golangci-lint v2 checks, and README/CONTRIBUTING docs.

title: Markdown all-version pages and field details
description: Finish the core Markdown renderer behavior: render all served versions in one page, add field detail anchors, and introduce first GitHub/Fern dialect differences beyond frontmatter.
acceptance_criteria: -o markdown* --all-versions renders every served version for CRD files and cluster resources; Markdown output includes field detail anchors; GitHub and Fern output differ in useful dialect-specific wrappers; CLI, renderer, and docs tests cover the behavior.
close_reason: Implemented all-version Markdown pages with field anchors and initial GitHub/Fern renderer differences.

title: Markdown column autodetection fallback
description: Markdown output can fall back to 80 columns even when the user's terminal has a wider width because only stdout is checked for terminal dimensions.
acceptance_criteria: Default Markdown column detection checks stdout, stderr, controlling terminal, and COLUMNS before falling back to 80; tests cover stderr and COLUMNS fallback paths.
close_reason: False alarm: output was piped into glow, so stdout was not a terminal and the 80-column fallback was expected behavior. Use --columns to override when piping.

title: Markdown paragraph wrapping
description: Wrap and reindent generated Markdown prose/YAML description comments to a target column width, defaulting to detected terminal width with a stable fallback, and expose --columns for explicit control.
acceptance_criteria: --columns <n> is wired through Cobra validation; Markdown rendering wraps schema description comments to the selected width while preserving YAML comment indentation and paragraph breaks; tests cover explicit column wrapping and CLI flag behavior.
close_reason: Implemented --columns validation and Markdown paragraph wrapping for YAML description comments, defaulting to detected terminal width with fallback 80, with renderer and CLI tests.

title: Interactive Fern MDX renderer
description: Add a Fern-specific interactive renderer target that emits MDX/custom component integration for foldable schema docs, using the same schema tree and search model as the generic HTML renderer where possible.
design: Design path: implement -o markdown-fern as static Fern MDX that is visually and behaviorally close to selected-resource HTML mode. Generated MDX should use Fern page components plus a reusable Fern-compatible schema component fed by a deterministic structured schema payload. Required interactions include per-field fold/unfold, focus details, keyboard navigation where practical, semantic comment wrapping, and filtering over field names/descriptions. Filtering is enabled by default and disabled with --disable-filtering. Fern output must not provide a dynamic realtime cluster overview or fetch OpenAPI after page load. First target is one selected resource; second target is static API group export with an index and one resource section per resource in the selected group.
acceptance_criteria: Generated markdown-fern output supports selected-resource export with Fern MDX/component markup for fold/unfold, focused details, and filtering by default. --disable-filtering removes filtering UI/indexes while preserving folding and details. The output embeds deterministic structured schema data, does not depend on a localhost kubectl-doc process, and does not fetch OpenAPI after page load. Tests cover single-resource output, filtering enabled/disabled, all-versions tabs, field metadata/details, MDX/JSX escaping, and the planned static API-group export shape.
close_reason: Implemented markdown-fern as static Fern MDX with embedded schema payload, KubeSchemaDoc component invocation, default filtering with --disable-filtering, all-version tabs, structured field metadata/details, MDX escaping, and renderer/CLI/Dynamo CRD tests.

title: OpenAPI example blobs in schema output
description: Preserve OpenAPI field examples from schema example/examples blobs and use them in generated documentation output when no default is present.
design: Extend the internal schema/documentation model to carry field-local examples from OpenAPI v3 and CRD schemas. YAML rendering should use an example value before enum or placeholder fallback when the example can be represented as valid YAML, and add compact inline metadata like # example string, # example object, or # example array. Rich renderers should expose the same example metadata in hover/focus/details surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Schema adapters preserve OpenAPI example/examples blobs. YAML output renders example values when no default is present and annotates them with # example <type>. Defaults still take precedence over examples. Tests cover scalar, object, array, named example, and default-over-example cases.
close_reason: Implemented internal example preservation for CRD and native OpenAPI schemas, YAML example rendering with default precedence, named example metadata, and tests for scalar, object, array, named, and default-over-example cases.

title: Path zoom for schema renderers
description: Add --path <json-path> so users can focus or zoom kubectl-doc output to a selected schema field after resource/version selection. The exact behavior is renderer-scoped: static text renderers may render the subtree as root, while interactive renderers keep the full schema and use the path as initial focus.
design: Resolve the requested JSON-path-like value against the normalized documentation tree, not raw OpenAPI. Static text renderers that support path zoom, initially yaml and markdown variants, render the selected subtree as the root of their schema output. Interactive renderers that support --path, including tui, browser, and html, keep the full selected schema available, expand the target ancestors, focus the selected field, show its details, and scroll it into view. Browser should preserve the requested path in the route or query string when practical. Renderers such as man and kro may reject --path until a useful renderer-specific behavior is implemented. The CLI should reject unsupported renderer/path combinations clearly.
acceptance_criteria: Cobra wires --path. Invalid paths produce a clear error after resource/version selection and schema normalization. Supported renderer/path combinations have renderer-specific tests: YAML/Markdown subtree zoom and HTML/browser/TUI initial focus semantics. Unsupported renderer/path combinations fail early with a clear error.

title: TUI renderer and shared search
description: Add interactive terminal documentation mode with split-screen field details, resource/version navigation, folding, and search.
design: Use -o tui and -i/--interactive for interactive terminal mode; -i switches the default renderer from yaml to tui and is not a YAML modifier. Implement the TUI with Bubble Tea v2, using Bubbles v2 components where they fit naturally for resource/version lists, viewport panes, and search input. Keep schema focus, fold state, search results, and field details backed by kubectl-doc's shared documentation/tree model, not by reparsing rendered YAML text. The active schema focus is always on one visible JSONPath field and the details view updates automatically. Up/Down move to the previous/next visible field. Left moves to the parent field. Right on a collapsed field expands it and moves to the first visible child; Right on other fields is a no-op. Tab and Shift-Tab jump between visible collapsible fields. Enter toggles the focused field when collapsible. Home/End move to first/last visible field. / searches field names and descriptions; // searches only field names; Esc exits search; n/p navigate highlighted results. q and F10 quit. Wide layouts place details on the right; narrower layouts place details below. Comment wrapping is automatic and semantic to the pane width, with YAML comment indentation and a fresh # on each wrapped visual line. If --path is provided, start with that field focused, ancestors expanded, and details visible.
acceptance_criteria: TUI starts in group/resource/version navigation when no resource is selected. Selected schemas show foldable YAML plus a responsive details pane: right side on wide screens and below on narrower screens. Up/Down move between visible fields; Left moves to parent; Right expands collapsed fields and jumps to first child; Tab and Shift-Tab jump between visible collapsible fields; Enter toggles; Home/End move to first/last field. Search highlights matches in strong orange and n/p navigate results. Comment wrapping is automatic and semantic. No y key binding and no copy command are present.
close_reason: Implemented the interactive TUI/search/filter milestone: TUI and browser overview navigation, schema focus/folding, slash search separation, incremental overview and schema filtering, structured-metadata rendering, keyboard behavior, and regression tests are in place.

title: Static HTML and browser output
description: Add static HTML output and browser mode for interactive foldable YAML documentation.
design: -o html writes a self-contained static interactive document to stdout for the selected CRD/resource and supports --all-versions for that selected resource. -o browser/-w starts a localhost server on port 0, prints the URL, and lives until Ctrl-C. In unfiltered cluster mode it serves discovery-backed group/resource/version navigation first and lazily fetches OpenAPI for the selected resource version instead of pre-rendering every cluster schema. Browser interaction mirrors text/TUI behavior where practical, including fold controls and search.
acceptance_criteria: -o html emits a static interactive HTML document. -o browser starts a localhost server on a random port and prints the URL. Browser mode supports folding and / search over field names and descriptions. No copy buttons or copy commands are added.
close_reason: Implemented static HTML output plus localhost browser mode with discovery landing, lazy schema routes, folding/search UI, and lifecycle tests.

title: Markdown documentation renderers
description: Add one-page Markdown output for documentation integration, with GitHub Markdown as the default Markdown dialect and Fern-specific support.
design: Build renderers on the shared documentation model. Support -o markdown as markdown-github, plus explicit markdown-github and markdown-fern dialects. Explore only GitHub and Fern dialect features for now.
acceptance_criteria: -o markdown and -o markdown-github render GitHub-friendly Markdown. -o markdown-fern renders Fern-compatible Markdown. Output is one page/file filtered by selected resource/group/version flags. Tests cover dialect differences.

title: Color YAML flow list punctuation
description: Make inline YAML list punctuation easier to read in colored output, especially overview version lists such as ["v2","v1"].
acceptance_criteria: Colored YAML output styles mapping colons plus [, ], and comma punctuation in flow lists with a light-grey syntax style. Tests cover the ANSI styling without changing plain YAML output.
close_reason: Colored YAML now renders mapping colons and flow-list punctuation with a light-grey syntax style.

title: Render multiple overview versions as YAML lists
description: Change cluster overview output so single-version resources render as a scalar version string, while multi-version resources render as a YAML flow sequence sorted latest-first.
acceptance_criteria: Single version remains 'pods: v1'. Multiple versions render like 'deployments: ["v1","v1beta1"]'. Version order prefers GA over beta over alpha, then higher numeric versions. Tests cover builder and renderer output.
close_reason: Overview versions now render as scalar for one version and latest-first YAML flow lists for multiple versions.

title: Color discovery overview output
description: Apply terminal color support to the YAML resource overview path when stdout supports color and --nocolor is not set.
acceptance_criteria: kubectl doc without -f uses the same color detection as CRD YAML rendering. Overview renderer has tests for plain and colored output.
close_reason: Overview YAML now uses the same terminal color support as schema YAML, with renderer coverage.
